sci-img-wood-w1x2.query: Beneath every forest floor lies a vast fungal internet. Mycorrhizal networks connect trees across acres, allowing them to share nutrients, water, and even chemical warning signals. Mother trees nurture their seedlings through this underground web. The Wood Wide Web: Fungal Networks
